articles.title: Nighttime Road Construction on Main Street Begins March 2
articles.summary: Crews will begin resurfacing Main Street this week, with nighttime lane closures and parking restrictions in downtown Maple Grove.
articles.body:
The Maple Grove public works department announced that nighttime road construction on Main Street will begin on March 2 and continue for approximately three weeks. Work will take place between 8 p.m. and 6 a.m. to minimize disruption to local businesses and daytime traffic.

During construction hours, at least one lane of Main Street will remain open in each direction, but drivers should expect delays and follow posted detours for cross-street access. On-street parking along the construction zone will be temporarily restricted. Residents are encouraged to use side streets and alternative routes where possible.

Project manager Carla Nguyen said crews will focus first on milling and resurfacing the roadway, followed by new striping and crosswalk markings. The town is also evaluating whether to add additional safety features, including improved crossings and better lighting.

articles.title: Road Construction Detour Planned Near Highway 7 This Weekend
articles.summary: Drivers heading to Highway 7 should prepare for a signed detour as crews replace an aging culvert under Oak Avenue.
articles.body:
Drivers traveling between Maple Grove and Highway 7 will encounter a new detour this weekend due to a culvert replacement project on Oak Avenue. The town announced that Oak Avenue will be fully closed between 3rd Street and the Highway 7 on-ramp from Friday at 7 p.m. through Sunday at 6 p.m.

A marked road construction detour will route traffic along Pine Street and County Road 12. Message boards and additional signage will be placed at key intersections to direct drivers. Local access to homes and businesses within the closure area will be maintained, but through-traffic will not be allowed.

Officials recommend that residents plan extra travel time, especially on Saturday morning when traffic volumes are typically higher. Emergency vehicles will have coordinated access through the work zone if needed.

articles.title: Lane Closures on Highway 7 Connector Start Monday
articles.summary: A resurfacing project on the Highway 7 connector will bring daytime lane closures for two weeks.
articles.body:
Beginning Monday, the Highway 7 connector between Maple Grove and neighboring Pine Ridge will undergo a resurfacing project expected to last two weeks. One lane will remain open in each direction, but drivers should be prepared for reduced speeds and intermittent delays.

The project is part of the county’s annual road construction program, which prioritizes routes with the highest traffic volumes and most urgent maintenance needs. Crews will also repair several potholes and replace damaged guardrails along the corridor.

Transportation officials encourage commuters to consider carpooling, using alternative routes, or shifting their travel times where possible during the construction period.

articles.title: Main Street Paving Project Wraps Up Ahead of Schedule
articles.summary: Contractors finished a month-long paving project on Main Street several days early, reopening all lanes to traffic.
articles.body:
The town announced that the Main Street paving project has been completed three days ahead of schedule, reopening all lanes to traffic and restoring on-street parking. The work included a full mill-and-overlay of the roadway surface, new crosswalk markings, and updated bike-friendly drainage grates.

Local business owners along Main Street expressed relief that the most disruptive phase of road construction is over. The public works department will monitor the new surface over the coming weeks and make minor adjustments as needed.

Officials thanked residents and visitors for their patience during the month-long project, which was funded in part by a state transportation grant.

articles.title: School Board Schedules Public Hearing on 2026–27 Budget
articles.summary: Residents are invited to comment on proposed school budget changes, including classroom upgrades and transportation costs.
articles.body:
The Maple Grove School Board will hold a public hearing on March 12 to gather input on the proposed 2026–27 budget. The draft plan includes funding for classroom technology upgrades, roof repairs at the middle school, and a modest increase in transportation costs due to rising fuel prices.

Residents may speak at the hearing, submit written comments in advance, or watch a livestream via the district’s website. Superintendent Alicia Romero said the board is “committed to transparency and community engagement” throughout the budget process.

A summary of the budget proposal is available online, and printed copies can be reviewed at the district office and public library.

articles.title: Farmers Market to Return Downtown on May 4
articles.summary: The popular Saturday farmers market will feature new vendors, live music, and expanded kids’ activities this season.
articles.body:
The Maple Grove Farmers Market will reopen for the season on Saturday, May 4, returning to its usual location in the Town Square parking lot. Organizers say more than 35 vendors have signed up, including local farms, bakeries, and artisans.

New this year are monthly "Kids at the Market" days with hands-on activities, cooking demos, and a produce passport program for families. Market manager Leo Martinez said the goal is to make the farmers market a welcoming place for all ages.

Live music, food trucks, and community organization booths will also be part of the weekly lineup. The market will run every Saturday from 8 a.m. to noon through October.

articles.title: Volunteers Sought for Elm Park Spring Cleanup
articles.summary: The Parks Department is organizing a community cleanup at Elm Park, with free lunch for volunteers.
articles.body:
The Maple Grove Parks Department is calling for volunteers to join a spring cleanup at Elm Park on March 21. Participants will help pick up litter, clear brush along walking paths, and spread fresh mulch around playgrounds and flower beds.

Families, scout troops, and neighborhood groups are encouraged to register in advance so organizers can plan supplies and refreshments. Gloves, trash bags, and tools will be provided, and volunteers will receive a free picnic lunch at the Elm Park pavilion.

Residents who can’t attend the event are invited to adopt a section of park or trail for ongoing stewardship throughout the year.

articles.title: Winter Storm Watch Issued for Thursday Night
articles.summary: Forecasters predict 4–7 inches of snow and gusty winds; town crews are preparing plows and treatment routes.
articles.body:
The National Weather Service has issued a winter storm watch for Maple Grove and surrounding areas from Thursday evening through Friday afternoon. Current forecasts call for 4–7 inches of snow, with higher amounts possible in localized bands.

Public works crews are pre-treating major roads and intersections, and residents are reminded to move parked vehicles off designated snow emergency routes if a full snow emergency is declared. Sidewalk clearing rules will also be in effect for residential and commercial properties.

The school district will announce any closures or delays by 6 a.m. Friday via email, text message, and local media outlets.

articles.title: Library Renovation Enters Final Phase
articles.summary: The Maple Grove Public Library is set to reopen its second floor next month, adding new study areas and a teen space.
articles.body:
Renovations at the Maple Grove Public Library are entering their final phase, with contractors completing work on the second floor and beginning interior finishing touches. The project includes new study rooms, a dedicated teen area, expanded children’s section, and improved accessibility throughout the building.

Library Director Hannah Patel said she expects the second floor to reopen to the public by late March, with a grand reopening celebration planned for April. During construction, core services have remained available on the first floor and online.

The renovation is funded by a combination of town bonds, state grants, and private donations from the Maple Grove Library Foundation.

articles.title: New Coffee Shop Opens on Main Street
articles.summary: Bean & Branch, a locally owned coffee shop, is now open on Main Street with an emphasis on fair trade beans.
articles.body:
Bean & Branch Coffee has opened its doors at 214 Main Street, filling a long-vacant storefront in downtown Maple Grove. The shop offers espresso drinks, drip coffee, teas, and a selection of pastries from local bakeries.

Owner Marcus Lee said he chose the Main Street location because of its walkability and proximity to other small businesses. The shop features reclaimed wood furnishings, plenty of natural light, and a small reading nook near the back.

Bean & Branch is open daily from 6:30 a.m. to 6 p.m., with extended hours during special events and festivals downtown.

articles.title: Town Launches Study on Adding Bike Lanes to Main Street
articles.summary: Planners will evaluate options for adding protected bike lanes along Main Street and connecting routes.
articles.body:
Maple Grove’s planning department has hired a consultant to study the feasibility of adding bike lanes along Main Street and several connecting corridors. The study will analyze traffic patterns, parking needs, and potential safety improvements for cyclists and pedestrians.

As part of the process, the town will host public workshops and online surveys to gather input from residents, business owners, and commuters. Planners will also review crash data and best practices from other communities that have implemented downtown bike lanes.

A draft set of recommendations is expected later this spring, with the Town Council to consider any proposed changes in early summer.

articles.title: Neighborhood Yard Sale Weekend Preview
articles.summary: Dozens of households plan to participate in this weekend’s community yard sale, with everything from toys to tools.
articles.body:
Maple Grove’s first neighborhood yard sale weekend of the year is set for March 7–8, and residents across town are getting ready. An interactive map of participating homes will be posted on the community website later this week, allowing shoppers to plan their routes.

Organizers encourage participants to clearly mark items and prices, and to consider donating unsold goods to local charities after the event. The town reminds residents to keep sidewalks clear and park responsibly to avoid blocking driveways or intersections.

If you’re hosting a sale, you can add your address and a brief description of items to the online listing through Friday evening.

articles.title: Maple Grove High Wins Regional Basketball Championship
articles.summary: The Tigers clinched the regional title with a 62–58 victory and advance to the state tournament.
articles.body:
In a packed gym on Friday night, the Maple Grove High School Tigers defeated the Lakeview Knights 62–58 to win the regional basketball championship. Senior guard Jamie Collins led all scorers with 24 points, including a crucial three-pointer in the final minute.

Coach Renée Thompson praised the team’s resilience and defense, noting that the Tigers held Lakeview to just eight points in the fourth quarter. The win sends Maple Grove to the state tournament for the first time in seven years.

A community send-off for the team is planned next week, with more details to be posted on the school’s website.

articles.title: Town Council Approves 2026 Operating Budget
articles.summary: The $24.3 million budget funds road maintenance, park improvements, and a new firefighter position.
articles.body:
After several weeks of public meetings and revisions, the Maple Grove Town Council unanimously approved the 2026 operating budget on Tuesday night. The $24.3 million plan includes increased funding for road maintenance, targeted park improvements, and the addition of a full-time firefighter/paramedic position.

Mayor Denise Clarke said the budget “strikes a balance between investing in core services and keeping taxes stable for residents.” The town also set aside money for future facility repairs and technology upgrades.

A detailed breakdown of the budget is available on the town’s website, and printed copies can be requested from the clerk’s office.

articles.title: Dog Safety Tips for Walks Near Elm Park
articles.summary: Local veterinarians share advice for keeping pets safe around busy streets and crowded park paths.
articles.body:
With warmer weather on the way, more residents are heading to Elm Park with their dogs. Local veterinarians are reminding pet owners to keep dogs on leashes, use secure collars or harnesses, and make sure ID tags and microchip information are up to date.

Elm Park borders several busy residential streets, and recent reports of dogs slipping out of yards or collars have prompted calls for extra caution. Experts also recommend reflective gear for evening walks and avoiding retractable leashes in crowded areas.

If you find a lost dog near Elm Park, the town advises contacting animal control or posting in the community’s Lost & Found section online so owners can be quickly reunited with their pets.

businesses.name: La Trattoria on Main
businesses.description: Cozy Italian restaurant on Main Street featuring house-made pasta, wood-fired pizzas, and a small but thoughtful wine list.
businesses.opening_hours: Mon–Thu 11:00 am–9:00 pm; Fri–Sat 11:00 am–10:00 pm; Sun 12:00 pm–8:00 pm

businesses.name: El Camino Taqueria
businesses.description: Casual Mexican taqueria known for street-style tacos, fresh salsas, and vegetarian options.

businesses.name: Main Street Pizzeria & Pasta
businesses.description: Family-friendly Italian spot serving New York–style pizza, pasta, and subs with a casual dine-in area.
businesses.opening_hours: Sun–Thu 11:00 am–9:00 pm; Fri–Sat 11:00 am–10:30 pm

businesses.opening_hours: Tue–Fri 10:00 am–6:00 pm; Sat 9:00 am–4:00 pm; Sun–Mon closed

classified_categories.description: Paid services such as lawn care, tutoring, repairs, and consulting.

classified_categories.name: Housing
classified_categories.description: Housing rentals, rooms, and home-share opportunities.

events.title: Family Nature Walk at Elm Park
events.description: Join park staff and volunteers for a guided family nature walk through Elm Park’s trails. We’ll look for early signs of spring, learn about local wildlife, and do a short scavenger hunt for kids.

events.title: Kids Craft Hour at Maple Grove Library
events.description: Drop in for a free craft session at the library. Staff and volunteers will help kids create simple spring-themed crafts. All materials are provided.

events.title: Sunday Afternoon Concert in the Park
events.description: Relax with an afternoon of live music from the Maple Grove Community Band at the Elm Park bandshell. Bring a blanket or lawn chairs.

events.title: Downtown Wine Tasting Evening
events.description: Sample a selection of regional wines and small bites at participating downtown businesses. Must be 21+ to attend.

events.title: Spring Into Action 5K Charity Run
events.description: A 5K run/walk to raise funds for the Maple Grove Community Food Pantry. Strollers and leashed dogs welcome.

events.title: Town Council Regular Meeting
events.description: The Maple Grove Town Council will hold its regular monthly meeting. Agenda items include road maintenance contracts and park improvements.

events.title: Farmers Market Opening Day
events.description: Celebrate the opening day of the Maple Grove Farmers Market with live music, kids’ activities, and a full lineup of local vendors.

events.title: Community Cleanup Day
events.description: Join neighbors to clean up litter along streets, parks, and streams throughout Maple Grove. Supplies and snacks provided.

events.title: Library Evening Book Club
events.description: Monthly evening book club meeting at the library. This month’s selection is a contemporary novel set in upstate New York.

events.title: Maple Grove High Spring Theater Production
events.description: The high school drama club presents its spring play. Tickets are available at the door and online.

events.title: Maple Grove Yard Sale Trail
events.description: Follow a route of participating homes across town holding yard and garage sales. Maps will be available online and at Town Hall.

events.title: Dog Park Meetup at Elm Park
events.description: Bring your pup to the Elm Park dog area for a casual meetup with other dog owners. Water and treats will be provided.

forum_categories.name: General Discussion
forum_categories.description: Talk about everyday life in Maple Grove, ask questions, and share local tips.

forum_categories.name: Town Improvements
forum_categories.description: Discuss road projects, bike lanes, parks, and long-term planning for Maple Grove.

forum_categories.name: Yard Sales & Garage Sales
forum_categories.description: Share upcoming yard and garage sales, tips for hosting, and bargain finds.

forum_categories.name: Farmers Market & Local Food
forum_categories.description: Talk about the Maple Grove Farmers Market, seasonal produce, and local food vendors.

forum_categories.name: Sports Talk
forum_categories.description: Chat about local school teams, adult leagues, and regional sports news.

classified_listings.title: Used Hybrid Commuter Bicycle - Large Frame
classified_listings.description: Gently used hybrid commuter bicycle with a large aluminum frame (19.5"). 21-speed Shimano drivetrain, fenders, and rear cargo rack included. Tuned up last fall at Riverfront Bike Shop and stored indoors over the winter. A good option for around-town rides and commuting to Main Street.

classified_listings.title: Used Kids' Bicycle 20" - Good Condition
classified_listings.description: Blue 20" kids' bicycle suitable for ages 7–10. Coaster brake plus front hand brake, kickstand, and bell. Light scuffs from normal use but rides well. Our child outgrew this bike last year. Pickup near Elm Park in Maple Grove.

classified_listings.title: Mountain Bike - Front Suspension, Disc Brakes
classified_listings.description: Medium-frame 27.5" mountain bike with front suspension fork and mechanical disc brakes. Bought two summers ago and used mostly on the rail trail. Recently tuned, new rear tire installed. Includes bottle cage and flat pedals.

classified_listings.title: Road Bicycle - Needs Work
classified_listings.description: Older steel-frame road bicycle from the 1990s. 56cm frame, drop bars, and 2x7 drivetrain. Needs new tires and a tune-up but could be a great project bike. Located in Pine Ridge, about 10–15 minutes from Maple Grove.

classified_listings.title: New City Bicycle with Basket
classified_listings.description: Brand-new city bicycle with step-through frame, 3-speed internal hub, and front basket. Bought online but realized the frame is too small for me. Never ridden except for a quick test in the driveway. Includes owner’s manual and reflectors.
classified_listings.location_text: Downtown Maple Grove, near Main Street

classified_listings.title: Trail Bicycle with Rack and Lights
classified_listings.description: Comfort-style trail bicycle with upright bars, rear cargo rack, and integrated lights. Recently serviced at Riverfront Bike Shop. Great for longer rides on the canal trail.

classified_listings.title: 4-Person Camping Tent - Lightly Used
classified_listings.description: Four-person dome camping tent used on two weekend trips. No holes or leaks, includes rainfly, stakes, and carrying bag. Selling because our family upgraded to a larger tent.

classified_listings.title: Lost golden retriever near Oak Street
classified_listings.description: Our family’s golden retriever, Daisy, slipped out of our backyard near Oak Street and 3rd Street on Monday evening around 7:30 pm. She is a medium-large dog (about 65 lbs), wearing a red collar with a bone-shaped tag, and is very friendly but may be nervous around traffic. She is microchipped. Please contact us if you see her anywhere near Oak Street or Elm Park.
classified_listings.location_text: Oak Street area, near Elm Park, Maple Grove

classified_listings.title: Found small white dog near Elm Park playground
classified_listings.description: Found a small white dog (looks like a terrier mix, around 15 lbs) near the Elm Park playground on Sunday afternoon around 3 pm. Wearing a blue collar but no visible tags. Very friendly and currently safe at our home a block from the park.

classified_listings.title: Lost gray tabby cat near Center Street
classified_listings.description: Our indoor gray tabby cat, Milo, slipped out the front door near Center Street and Maple Avenue on Friday night. He is a medium-sized cat with green eyes and a striped tail, wearing a green collar with a bell. He is shy but food-motivated. Please check under porches and in garages.
classified_listings.location_text: Center Street & Maple Avenue, Maple Grove

classified_listings.title: Found set of keys near Town Hall Plaza
classified_listings.description: Found a small set of keys (two house keys and a car key fob) on a lanyard near the steps of Maple Grove Town Hall on Tuesday morning. Describe the keychain or car brand to claim.

classified_listings.title: Free 3-seat sofa - must pick up
classified_listings.description: Dark gray 3-seat sofa, still comfortable but fabric shows some wear on the arms. No major stains, from a smoke-free home. Free to anyone who can pick it up from our house near Oak Avenue this week.

classified_listings.title: Spring lawn care and mowing service
classified_listings.description: Offering weekly lawn mowing, trimming, and spring clean-up services in Maple Grove starting in April. Small local business with reliable equipment and references available. Most small yards start around $40 per visit.

classified_listings.title: Room for rent near Center Street
classified_listings.description: Furnished room for rent in a quiet home near Center Street and the library. Shared kitchen and bathroom, off-street parking. Looking for a responsible tenant, month-to-month lease.

classified_listings.title: Weekend moving help and small hauling
classified_listings.description: Two-person team with a pickup truck available on weekends for small moves, furniture delivery, and hauling items to the transfer station. Ideal for yard sale leftovers, donated furniture, or small apartment moves.

classified_listings.title: Lost brown leather wallet on Main Street
classified_listings.description: I lost a brown leather wallet somewhere along Main Street between Bean & Branch Coffee and Town Hall on Wednesday afternoon. It contains my ID and several cards, no cash. If found, please contact me—no questions asked.

polls.question: What new feature would you most like to see at the Maple Grove Farmers Market this season?
Which weekend works best for the spring community yard sale?
Should Maple Grove adopt an overnight winter parking ban during snow season?
What leash rule would you prefer for the Elm Park dog area?

forum_topics.title: Farmers Market 2026: Vendor Mix and New Ideas
forum_topics.body:
The Maple Grove Farmers Market opening day is coming up soon, and the organizers have asked for community feedback on what youd like to see this season. Are there specific kinds of farms, bakers, prepared foods, or artisan vendors you hope will be there?

Please share your thoughts on:
- What types of vendors youd like more of (produce, dairy, baked goods, hot food, crafts, etc.)
- Any services or amenities were missing (shade tents, seating, kids activities)
- Ideas for music or special market themes

Also, dont forget to vote in the poll attached to this thread about what new feature should be the top priority this year.

forum_topics.title: Farmers Market Favorites: What Do You Always Buy?
forum_topics.body:
With the Maple Grove Farmers Market reopening in May, I thought it would be fun to start a thread on everyones must-have items. Is there a particular farm stand, bakery, or prepared-food vendor you never skip? Any hidden-gem products that new visitors should know about?

Share your favorites, plus any tips on when to arrive for the best selection (and shortest lines).

forum_topics.title: Parking and Traffic Tips for the Farmers Market
forum_topics.body:
Last year the Saturday morning traffic near the Town Square parking lot got pretty hectic on busy market days. For folks who have been going for a few seasons, what are your best tips for parking and getting in and out safely?

Do you prefer street parking, biking, walking, or using nearby lots? Any intersections that feel especially tricky when the market is packed?

forum_topics.title: Spring Yard Sale Weekend 2026  Date Poll & Planning Thread
forum_topics.body:
Its time to pick a date for this years community yard sale weekend in Maple Grove. Last year we had over 60 participating households around town.

Please cast your vote in the attached poll for which weekend works best, and use this thread to:
- Say if you plan to host a yard or garage sale
- Share what part of town youre in
- Suggest ways to improve maps, signage, and traffic flow

Once a weekend is chosen, well pin a separate thread for sharing addresses and item highlights.

forum_topics.title: Best Tips for Pricing Items at Yard Sales
forum_topics.body:
For those who have hosted yard or garage sales in Maple Grove before: how do you decide what to charge for common items like kids clothes, toys, small appliances, and furniture?

Do you prefer to label everything individually, group similar items together with one price, or just use make an offer signs? Any advice on what tends to sell well vs. what people usually pass over?

forum_topics.title: Share Your Address for the Yard Sale Map
forum_topics.body:
Once the community picks a weekend for the spring yard sale, well be creating an online map of participating homes.

If you plan to host a sale, please reply here with:
- Your street (you can omit the exact house number if you prefer until closer to the date)
- The days and hours you expect to be open
- A short list of featured items (e.g., kids clothes 2T7, tools, small furniture)

Well compile everything into one map and printable list.

forum_topics.title: Should Maple Grove Adopt an Overnight Winter Parking Ban?
forum_topics.body:
Town staff have mentioned that one option to improve snow plowing this coming winter is an overnight parking ban on certain streets during snow season. Some nearby towns already do this, while Maple Grove currently relies on voluntary compliance and temporary snow emergency declarations.

Supporters say a consistent overnight ban makes it easier to clear roads curb-to-curb and improves safety. Opponents worry about limited off-street parking and the impact on renters.

Please share how an overnight winter parking ban would affect you and your neighborhood, and vote in the attached poll to let the council know where you stand.

forum_topics.title: Lowering the Speed Limit on Main Street Downtown?
forum_topics.body:
With more people walking, biking, and visiting new businesses on Main Street, several residents have asked whether the speed limit through the downtown stretch should be reduced from 30 mph to 25 mph.

If you live, work, or drive through this area regularly, what do you think? Would a lower limit make a noticeable difference in safety or noise, or would it just slow traffic without much benefit?

Please include which part of town youre referencing and any ideas for additional safety measures (crosswalks, curb bump-outs, etc.).

forum_topics.title: Crosswalk Improvements Near Elm Park
forum_topics.body:
The crosswalks around Elm Park, especially near the playground and parking lot entrances, feel a bit chaotic during busy times. Families with strollers and kids on bikes are crossing while cars are looking for parking.

Would you support things like high-visibility crosswalk paint, flashing pedestrian signs, or speed humps near the park entrances? Please share any near-miss stories or suggestions for specific intersections that need attention.

forum_topics.title: Elm Park Dog Area: Leash Rules and Courtesy
forum_topics.body:
With more people using the Elm Park dog area once the weather warms up, there has been some confusion about when dogs must be leashed and how the fenced area is supposed to be used.

Some owners would like more off-leash time for dogs to run, while others are concerned about kids and seniors walking through the park. The Parks Department is reviewing the current rules and signage.

Please share your experiences at the Elm Park dog areagood or badand vote in the attached poll on what leash rule youd prefer going forward.

forum_topics.title: Best Italian or Mexican Restaurants Near 12345?
forum_topics.body:
Were new to Maple Grove and looking for good Italian or Mexican restaurants within a short drive of the 12345 ZIP code. So far weve heard great things about La Trattoria on Main and El Camino Taqueria.

Which places are your favorites for pasta, pizza, tacos, or burritos? Any hidden gems for takeout or family-friendly dinners? Please mention if they tend to be busy and whether theyre good for kids.

forum_topics.title: Tigers Win Regional Basketball Championship!
forum_topics.body:
What a game! Maple Grove High pulled off a huge win over Lakeview to take the regional title. Jamie Collins hitting that late three-pointer was incredible.

Who else was there in person or watching the stream? Favorite moments from the game? Also, any predictions for how far the Tigers can go in the state tournament?

forum_topics.title: Spring Pickup Soccer at Elm Park
forum_topics.body:
A few of us are interested in starting a casual pickup soccer game at Elm Park once the fields dry out. Likely Sunday afternoons, mixed ages and skill levels.

If youre interested, reply with your preferred times and whether you have extra cones or a portable goal to bring. Families and teens welcome, we just want to keep it fun and low-pressure.

forum_topics.title: How Do You Prefer to Receive Emergency Alerts and Road Closure Notices?
forum_topics.body:
The town is reviewing how it sends out emergency alerts (like winter storms) and road closure notices for construction. Right now we use a mix of email, text messages, social media, and banners at the top of this site.

What works best for you? Do you check on-site notifications here regularly, or do you rely more on email and text? Any ideas to make sure important alerts arent missed?

forum_posts.body: Id love to see more actual produce vendors earlier in the season. Last year the first few weeks were heavy on baked goods and crafts, but not many greens. A couple of farms that focus on early spinach, lettuce, and radishes would be amazing.

More shade and seating, please! The kids love the market but there arent many places for grandparents to sit while we shop. A few pop-up shade tents with benches or picnic tables near the music area would go a long way.
Im hoping for at least one more dairy vendor  someone selling yogurt and maybe farm-fresh cheese. Also a stand with more gluten-free baked goods would be great for those of us with allergies.
Hot breakfast options! A breakfast burrito or omelet stand using market eggs and veggies would be a huge hit for early shoppers. Right now its mostly coffee and pastries.

Id like to see rotating theme days  one Saturday focused on kids activities, another on local artisans, another on live music. It might help spread out the crowds instead of everything happening on opening day.

Seconding this  I usually go right when they open and its mostly bread and cookies in May. It would be awesome if the market could coordinate with a couple of greenhouse growers for early-season produce to balance things out.
A dedicated kids tent with simple craft projects and maybe a farm of the week activity would be great. Last year the produce passport days were packed  more of that please!
Id like to see at least one vendor focusing on bulk pantry items: beans, grains, oats, maybe even spices. It would be nice to restock staples without another trip to the grocery store.
Any chance of a knife sharpening service visiting once a month? Ive seen that at other markets and its really convenient to bring kitchen knives and garden tools in while you shop.
More information boards would help  a big sign at the entrance listing whats in season that week and where to find it. New visitors sometimes dont realize there are vendors around the back row.

Were on Pine Street near the elementary school and plan to participate again this year. Either of the first two weekends in May would work for us. Well mostly have kids clothes, toys, and some kitchen gadgets.

Oak Avenue block here  wed prefer the later weekend so we have more time to clean out the garage. Last year the Saturday-only folks were slammed in the morning, so were thinking of doing both days this time.

Dont waste your weekend running a sale yourself  hire our PRO estate and yard sale team! We photograph, price, and advertise your items and handle the whole weekend for you. Packages start at just $199 and include social media ads and haul-away. Call 555-0123 today to book your spot!
Im over by Elm Park and can do either weekend, but I voted for the same one as last year so shoppers can get in the habit. We have a bunch of baby gear and a lawn mower to put out.

Id support keeping the main paths on-leash but allowing off-leash inside the fenced area only. Clearer signs at the entrances would help a lot  right now its easy to miss the rules if you come in from the parking lot side.
Agree with this. Maybe add one more waste bag station by the playground entrance too. Its not fun when people cut across the grass with dogs off-leash and kids are playing nearby.

La Trattoria on Main is our go-to for date night Italian  the pasta is great and theyre surprisingly kid-friendly early in the evening. For Mexican, El Camino Taqueria has the best tacos in town, especially the carne asada and the veggie tacos.
Main Street Pizzeria & Pasta is great for quick, affordable dinners with kids. Not fancy, but the pizza is solid and theyre very used to families coming in after games.
